I use numpy to implement a softmax function with the below code. I get zero after using numpy.exp. In theory, the return of exp function is always larger than 0.
x - max_x 
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b7e92549-6ba5-422f-996d-e47121a25af3)

exp_x
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5bb3de33-4309-4725-9aa2-92a3d68cecc0)

However, if I use np.exp(-121.294205), I can get the correct result


### Reproduce the code example:

```python
max_x = np.max(x, axis=1, keepdims=True)
exp_x = np.exp(x - max_x)
print("x - max_x", x - max_x)
print("exp_x ", exp_x)
```

python version:  3.8.1
numpy version:  1.23.4
